sections.label: Section 1.475
sections.original: φοιτᾷ γὰρ ὑπʼ ἀγρίαν
sections.translation: ordering all to search for the unknown man . He wanders under cover of the wild wood , among caves and rocks , fierce as a bull , wretched and forlorn on his joyless path , still seeking to separate himself from the doom revealed at the central shrine of the earth .

sections.label: Section 1.480
sections.original: τὰ μεσόμφαλα γᾶς ἀπονοσφίζων
sections.translation: But that doom lives forever , forever flits around him .

sections.label: Section 2.483
sections.original: δεινὰ μὲν οὖν , δεινὰ ταράσσει σοφὸς οἰωνοθέτας
sections.translation: The wise augur moves me , neither approving nor denying , with dread , with dread indeed .

sections.label: Section 2.485
sections.original: οὔτε δοκοῦντʼ οὔτʼ ἀποφάσκονθʼ · ὅ τι λέξω δʼ ἀπορῶ .
sections.translation: I am at a loss what to say . I am flustered in my expectations , seeing neither the present nor the future clearly . Never in past days or in these have I heard how the house of Labdacus or the son of Polybus had any quarrel with one another that

sections.label: Section 2.490
sections.original: ἢ τῷ Πολύβου νεῖκος ἔκειτʼ , οὔτε πάροιθέν ποτʼ ἔγωγʼ
sections.translation: I could bring as proof in assailing the public reputation of Oedipus , seeking to avenge the line of Labdacus

sections.label: Section 2.495
sections.original: ἐπίκουρος ἀδήλων θανάτων .
sections.translation: for the undiscovered murder .

sections.label: Section 2.496
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὁ μὲν οὖν Ζεὺς ὅ τʼ Ἀπόλλων ξυνετοὶ καὶ τὰ βροτῶν
sections.translation: Zeus and Apollo indeed are keen of thought and know the affairs of the earth .

sections.label: Section 2.500
sections.original: εἰδότες · ἀνδρῶν δʼ ὅτι μάντις πλέον ἢ ʼγὼ φέρεται ,
sections.translation: But there is no sure test of whether a mortal seer attains more knowledge than I do , though man may surpass man in wisdom . But until I see the word made good , I will never assent when men blame Oedipus . Before all eyes the winged maiden came against him once upon a time , and he was seen to be wise ,

sections.label: Section 2.510
sections.original: ποτέ , καὶ σοφὸς ὤφθη βασάνῳ θʼ ἁδύπολις τῷ ἀπʼ ἐμᾶς
sections.translation: and bore the test in welcome service to the state . Never , therefore , will he be adjudged guilty of evil .

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: ἄνδρες πολῖται , δείνʼ ἔπη πεπυσμένος
sections.translation: Fellow citizens , having learned that Oedipus the king lays dire charges against me , I have come in indignation .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: πάρειμʼ ἀτλητῶν . εἰ γὰρ ἐν ταῖς ξυμφοραῖς
sections.translation: If he thinks that in the present troubles he has suffered from me , by word or by deed , anything harmful , truly I do not desire my full term of years , when I must bear such blame as this . The wrong of this rumor

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἡ ζημία μοι τοῦ λόγου τούτου φέρει ,
sections.translation: touches me not in one point alone , but has the largest scope , if I am to be called a traitor in the city , a traitor by you and by my friends .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦλθε μὲν δὴ τοῦτο τοὔνειδος τάχʼ ἂν
sections.translation: But perhaps this taunt came under the stress of anger , rather than from the purpose of his heart .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: τοὔπος δʼ ἐφάνθη , ταῖς ἐμαῖς γνώμαις ὅτι
sections.translation: Was the opinion given that my counsels had brought the seer to utter his falsehoods ?

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: ηὐδᾶτο μὲν τάδʼ , οἶδα δʼ οὐ γνώμῃ τίνι .
sections.translation: Such things were said — I do not know with what meaning .

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: κατηγορεῖτο τοὐπίκλημα τοῦτό μου ;
sections.translation: And was this charge laid against me with steady eyes and steady mind ?

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἃ γὰρ δρῶσʼ , οἱ κρατοῦντες οὐχ ὁρῶ .
sections.translation: I do not know . I see not what my masters do . But here comes our lord from the house .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: οὗτος σύ , πῶς δεῦρʼ ἦλθες ; ἦ τοσόνδʼ ἔχεις
sections.translation: You , how did you get here ? Are you so boldfaced that you have come to my house , you who are manifestly the murderer of its master ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: λῃστής τʼ ἐναργὴς τῆς ἐμῆς τυραννίδος ;
sections.translation: the palpable thief of its crown ? Come , tell me , in the name of the gods , was it cowardice or folly which you saw in me and which led you to plot this thing ? Did you think that I would not notice this deed of yours creeping upon me by stealth , or that if I became aware of it I would not ward it off ?

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἆρʼ οὐχὶ μῶρόν ἐστι τοὐγχείρημά σου ,
sections.translation: Is your attempt not foolish , to seek the throne without followers or friends — a prize which followers and wealth must win ?

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: οἶσθʼ ὡς πόησον ; ἀντὶ τῶν εἰρημένων
sections.translation: Mark me now : hear a fair reply in answer to your words , and then judge for yourself on the basis of knowledge .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: λέγειν σὺ δεινός , μανθάνειν δʼ ἐγὼ κακὸς
sections.translation: You are apt in speech , but I have a poor wit for lessons , since I have found you a malignant foe .

sections.label: Section 547
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτὸ νῦν μου πρῶτʼ ἄκουσον ὡς ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: Now hear first how I will explain this very thing .

sections.label: Section 548
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτὸ μή μοι φράζʼ , ὅπως οὐκ εἶ κακός .
sections.translation: One thing — that you are not false — do not bother to explain to me .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: εἴ τοι νομίζεις κτῆμα τὴν αὐθαδίαν
sections.translation: If you think that stubbornness

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: εἶναί τι τοῦ νοῦ χωρίς , οὐκ ὀρθῶς φρονεῖς .
sections.translation: without sense is a virtue , you are not wise .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: εἴ τοι νομίζεις ἄνδρα συγγενῆ κακῶς
sections.translation: If you think you can wrong a kinsman and escape the penalty , you are not sane .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: ξύμφημί σοι ταῦτʼ ἔνδικʼ εἰρῆσθαι · τὸ δὲ
sections.translation: Justly said , I grant you : but tell me what the wrong is that you say you are suffering at my hands .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: ἔπειθες ἢ οὐκ ἔπειθες , ὡς χρείη μʼ ἐπὶ
sections.translation: Did you , or did you not , advise me to send for that reverend seer ?

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἔθʼ αὑτός εἰμι τῷ βουλεύματι .
sections.translation: Even now I am of the same mind .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: πόσον τινʼ ἤδη δῆθʼ ὁ Λάϊος χρόνον
sections.translation: How long is it , then , since Laius . . .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: δέδρακε ποῖον ἔργον ; οὐ γὰρ ἐννοῶ .
sections.translation: Did what ? I do not understand .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἄφαντος ἔρρει θανασίμῳ χειρώματι ;
sections.translation: was swept out of sight by deadly violence ?

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: μακροὶ παλαιοί τʼ ἂν μετρηθεῖεν χρόνοι .
sections.translation: The count of years would run far into the past .

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: τότʼ οὖν ὁ μάντις οὗτος ἦν ἐν τῇ τέχνῃ ;
sections.translation: Did this seer possess this skill in those days ?

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: σοφός γʼ ὁμοίως κἀξ ἴσου τιμώμενος .
sections.translation: He was wise as now , and held in equal honor .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: ἐμνήσατʼ οὖν ἐμοῦ τι τῷ τότʼ ἐν χρόνῳ ;
sections.translation: Did he make mention of me at that time ?

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐμοῦ γʼ ἑστῶτος οὐδαμοῦ πέλας .
sections.translation: Never , certainly , when I was within hearing .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἔρευναν τοῦ κτανόντος ἔσχετε ;
sections.translation: Did you never investigate the murder ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: παρέσχομεν , πῶς δʼ οὐχί ; κοὐκ ἠκούσαμεν .
sections.translation: Due search we made , of course , though we learned nothing .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: πῶς οὖν τόθʼ οὗτος ὁ σοφὸς οὐκ ηὔδα τάδε ;
sections.translation: And how was it that this sage did not tell this story then .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἐφʼ οἷς γὰρ μὴ φρονῶ σιγᾶν φιλῶ .
sections.translation: I do not know : where I lack insight it is my custom to be silent .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: τοσόνδε γʼ οἶσθα καὶ λέγοις ἂν εὖ φρονῶν .
sections.translation: This much , at least , you know , and could declare with insight enough .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: ποῖον τόδʼ ; εἰ γὰρ οἶδά γʼ , οὐκ ἀρνήσομαι .
sections.translation: What is that ? If I know it , I will not deny it .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: ὁθούνεκʼ , εἰ μὴ σοὶ ξυνῆλθε , τάσδʼ ἐμὰς
sections.translation: That , if he had not conferred with you , he would not have named me as Laius ’ slayer .

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: εἰ μὲν λέγει τάδʼ , αὐτὸς οἶσθʼ · ἐγὼ δὲ σοῦ
sections.translation: If he says this , you know best : but I deem it just

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: μαθεῖν δικαιῶ ταὔθʼ ἅπερ κἀμοῦ σὺ νῦν .
sections.translation: to learn from you as much as you have from me now .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: ἐκμάνθανʼ · οὐ γὰρ δὴ φονεὺς ἁλώσομαι .
sections.translation: Learn your fill . I will never be found guilty of the bloodshed .

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ; ἀδελφὴν τὴν ἐμὴν γήμας ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Say , then — have you married my sister ?

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: ἄρνησις οὐκ ἔνεστιν ὧν ἀνιστορεῖς .
sections.translation: The question allows no denial .

sections.label: Section 579
sections.original: ἄρχεις δʼ ἐκείνῃ ταὐτὰ γῆς ἴσον νέμων ;
sections.translation: And you rule the land as she does , with equal sway .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: ἃν ᾖ θέλουσα πάντʼ ἐμοῦ κομίζεται .
sections.translation: She obtains from me all that she wishes .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: οὔκουν ἰσοῦμαι σφῷν ἐγὼ δυοῖν τρίτος ;
sections.translation: And do I not rank as a third peer of you two ?

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα γὰρ δὴ καὶ κακὸς φαίνει φίλος .
sections.translation: It is just in this that you are seen to be a false friend .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: οὔκ , εἰ διδοίης γʼ ὡς ἐγὼ σαυτῷ λόγον .
sections.translation: Not so , if you would reason with your heart as I do with mine . Weigh this first — whether you think that anyone would

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἄρχειν ἑλέσθαι ξὺν φόβοισι μᾶλλον ἢ
sections.translation: choose to rule amid terrors rather than in unruffled peace , granted that he is to have the same powers . Now I , for one , have by nature no yearning to rule as a king rather than to do kingly deeds , and neither does any man I know who has a sound mind .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: νῦν μὲν γὰρ ἐκ σοῦ πάντʼ ἄνευ φόβου φέρω ,
sections.translation:
For now I attain all everything from you without fear , but , if I were ruler myself , I would have to do much that went against my own pleasure .
How , then , could royalty be sweeter to me to have than painless rule and influence ? I am not yet so misguided

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἄλλα χρῄζειν ἢ τὰ σὺν κέρδει καλά .
sections.translation: that I desire other honors than those which bring profit . Now , every man has a greeting for me ; now , all that have a request of you crave to speak with me , since in me lies all their hope of success . Why then should I give up these things and take those others ?

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν γένοιτο νοῦς κακὸς καλῶς φρονῶν .
sections.translation: No mind will become false while it is wise . No , I am no lover of such a policy , and if another put it into action , I could never bear to go along with him . And , in proof of this , first go to Pytho , and ask whether I brought a true report of the oracle .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ἄλλʼ , ἐάν με τῷ τερασκόπῳ λάβῃς
sections.translation: Then next , if you have found that I have planned anything in concert with the soothsayer , take and slay me , by the sentence not of one mouth , but of two — by my own no less than yours . But do not assume my guilt on unproven inference . It is not just to judge bad men good at random ,
